title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: **AI Video Language Transformati…
cards.body: **AI Video Language Transformation** converts Chinese-language GitHub tutorial videos into English while preserving the original visual track and cloning the speaker's voice.

cards.title: The goal is simple to describe a…
cards.body: The goal is simple to describe and hard to execute: same video, new language, familiar speaker identity.

cards.title: The system chains together trans…
cards.body: The system chains together transcription, translation, speaker diarization, speech synthesis, audio alignment, and video merging.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: Each stage has to work, but the …
cards.body: Each stage has to work, but the final quality depends on how well the stages hand timing, speaker identity, terminology, and audio constraints to one another.
![AI video language transformation pipeline diagram](/media/gifs/from-chinese-to-english-and-a-detour-into-moroccan-arabic/pipeline-2/diagram.gif)

cards.title: After the Chinese-to-English pip…
cards.body: After the Chinese-to-English pipeline worked, the project took a harder detour: testing what it would take to fine-tune voice cloning for Darija, Moroccan Arabic.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: That experiment did not produce …
cards.body: That experiment did not produce a production-ready voice model, but it exposed the real bottlenecks behind low-resource speech synthesis.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: The main pipeline chains togethe…
cards.body: The main pipeline chains together several components, each handling a distinct task:

cards.title: **Transcription** — [faster-whis…
cards.body: 1.**Transcription** — [faster-whisper](https://github.com/SYSTRAN/faster-whisper) (large-v3) converts the Chinese audio into timed text segments 2.**Translation** — [DeepL](https://www.

cards.title: deepl.
cards.body: deepl.com/) translates Chinese segments to English, with a [Gemini](https://deepmind.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: google/technologies/gemini/) rev…
cards.body: google/technologies/gemini/) review pass for technical terminology (GitHub-specific vocabulary like "pull request", "commit", "branch") 3.**Speaker Diarization** — [pyannote.

cards.title: audio](https://github.
cards.body: audio](https://github.com/pyannote/pyannote-audio) identifies who is speaking and when, enabling per-speaker voice cloning 4.**Speech Synthesis** — [F5-TTS](https://github.

cards.title: com/SWivid/F5-TTS) and [Voxtral]…
cards.body: com/SWivid/F5-TTS) and [Voxtral](https://mistral.ai/news/voxtral) generate English audio in the original speaker's voice 5.**Audio Alignment** — [pyrubberband](https://github.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: com/bmcfee/pyrubberband) time-st…
cards.body: com/bmcfee/pyrubberband) time-stretches segments that are too long, preserving pitch; [pyloudnorm](https://github.

cards.title: com/csteinmetz1/pyloudnorm) norm…
cards.body: com/csteinmetz1/pyloudnorm) normalizes loudness to broadcast standard (-14 LUFS) 6.**Video Merge** — [FFmpeg](https://ffmpeg.org/) replaces the original audio track with the new English one

cards.title: The result: a video that looks i…
cards.body: The result: a video that looks identical to the original, but speaks English — in the same voice.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: A [Streamlit](https://streamlit.
cards.body: A [Streamlit](https://streamlit.io/) web interface ties it all together, letting you monitor each stage, review individual segments, edit translations, and inspect quality metrics.
![Project explainer GIF](/media/gifs/from-chinese-to-english-and-a-detour-into-moroccan-arabic/pipeline-1/01-project-overview.gif)

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: The most technically demanding p…
cards.body: The most technically demanding piece of the pipeline is voice cloning — and it is worth dwelling on why.

cards.title: Commercial TTS systems give you …
cards.body: Commercial TTS systems give you a library of pre-made voices.You pick one and type your text.

cards.title: That is convenient but fundament…
cards.body: That is convenient but fundamentally limited: the voice is fictional, and you lose the identity of the original speaker.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: Zero-shot voice cloning is diffe…
cards.body: Zero-shot voice cloning is different.Models like F5-TTS can synthesize speech in an arbitrary speaker's voice using only a short reference audio clip.

cards.title: They learn not just *how to spea…
cards.body: They learn not just *how to speak*, but *how a specific person speaks* — their timbre, their rhythm, their breath patterns.

cards.title: Feed it a clip of someone, and i…
cards.body: Feed it a clip of someone, and it will generate new sentences in that voice.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: This is powerful.
cards.body: This is powerful.It is also hard to get right across multiple speakers, across videos, across different recording conditions.

cards.title: Our pipeline uses speaker diariz…
cards.body: Our pipeline uses speaker diarization to extract clean reference audio per speaker, enabling consistent voice identity throughout the output.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: After completing the main pipeli…
cards.body: After completing the main pipeline, we asked a natural question: what would it take to apply this to a language that barely exists in any TTS model's training data?

cards.title: That question pointed directly a…
cards.body: That question pointed directly at Darija — Moroccan Arabic — for reasons that were both personal and technical.

cards.title: On the personal side: Darija is …
cards.body: On the personal side: Darija is the language of everyday life in Morocco.It is the language of family conversations, street markets, and voice messages between friends.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: The idea that AI speech systems …
cards.body: The idea that AI speech systems largely do not support it — while supporting dozens of European languages with far fewer speakers — felt worth pushing against.

cards.title: On the technical side: Darija is…
cards.body: On the technical side: Darija is a genuine research challenge.

cards.title: It is a low-resource dialect wit…
cards.body: It is a low-resource dialect with deep structural differences from Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) — different phonology, a vocabulary that borrows heavily from Amazigh, French, and Spanish, and a rapid consonant-heavy rhythm.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: It can be written in Arabic scri…
cards.body: It can be written in Arabic script or in Arabizi (a Latin-numeric hybrid where, for example, **3afak** represents **عفاك** — the 3 standing for the Arabic letter ع).

cards.title: Neither form is standardized.
cards.body: Neither form is standardized.

cards.title: When Arabic script is used, shor…
cards.body: When Arabic script is used, short vowels are omitted, as is standard in Arabic writing; vowel diacritics (tashkeel) would help a TTS model but are rarely used in practice and hard to collect at scale.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: Before describing our experiment…
cards.body: Before describing our experiment, it is worth situating it within what already exists.

cards.title: Commercial services like SpeechG…
cards.body: Commercial services like SpeechGen.io offer native Moroccan Arabic voices — "Mouna" (female) and "Jamal" (male) — explicitly tuned to Darija.

cards.title: These work well for marketing vi…
cards.body: These work well for marketing videos or radio promotions.But they use predefined voices.You cannot clone an arbitrary speaker with them.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: The open-source landscape is mor…
cards.body: The open-source landscape is more recent and more interesting.Hugging Face spaces like [medmac01/Darija-Arabic-TTS](https://huggingface.

cards.title: co/spaces/medmac01/Darija-Arabic…
cards.body: co/spaces/medmac01/Darija-Arabic-TTS) fine-tune XTTS checkpoints for Darija.HAMMALE's speecht5-darija adapts Microsoft's SpeechT5 for Moroccan Arabic.

cards.title: Most recently, the "Habibi" fram…
cards.body: Most recently, the "Habibi" framework (2026) adapted F5-TTS itself to cover 12 regional Arabic dialects using a curriculum training strategy — starting from MSA data and gradually shifting to dialect-specific speech.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: The datasets remain the bottlene…
cards.body: The datasets remain the bottleneck.The premier open-source TTS corpus for Darija is DODa (AtlasIA): roughly 9 hours of speech across 7 speakers, with text in Arabic, Latin, and English.

cards.title: Most other large Darija corpora …
cards.body: Most other large Darija corpora (MGB-5, MoulSot, Casablanca) were built for speech recognition, not synthesis, and lack the standardized orthography synthesis models need.

cards.title: This data situation shapes every…
cards.body: This data situation shapes everything that follows.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: F5-TTS's base vocabulary contain…
cards.body: F5-TTS's base vocabulary contains 2,545 character tokens. Arabic characters are not among them. Before any fine-tuning could begin, we had to extend the model's input layer.

cards.title: The process:
cards.body: The process:

cards.title: **Vocabulary extension** — colle…
cards.body: 1.**Vocabulary extension** — collect every unique character from the Darija dataset, append the new Arabic ones to the base vocab file 2.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: **Embedding resize** — the model…
cards.body: **Embedding resize** — the model's text embedding matrix needs a new row for each added character.

cards.title: We initialized new rows with ran…
cards.body: We initialized new rows with random noise scaled to the standard deviation of existing embeddings, so the model starts with a reasonable prior rather than zeros 3.

cards.title: **Fine-tuning** — train on 20,00…
cards.body: **Fine-tuning** — train on 20,000 samples from [DarijaTTS-clean](https://huggingface.co/datasets/KandirResearch/DarijaTTS-clean) on an H100 GPU in Google Colab

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: ```python old_emb = state_dict['…
cards.body: ```python old_emb = state_dict['ema_model.transformer.text_embed.text_embed.weight'] new_emb = torch.zeros(new_vocab_size, emb_dim) new_emb[:old_size] = old_emb std = old_emb.std().

cards.title: item() new_emb[old_size:] = torch.
cards.body: item() new_emb[old_size:] = torch.randn(new_vocab_size - old_size, emb_dim) * std ```

cards.title: The acoustic architecture — the …
cards.body: The acoustic architecture — the flow-matching model, the voice cloning mechanism — was preserved entirely. Only the text input representation changed.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: The fine-tuned model did not pro…
cards.body: The fine-tuned model did not produce intelligible Darija.The output had structure — rhythm, voice, the shape of speech — but no recognizable phonemes.

cards.title: The model was clearly generating…
cards.body: The model was clearly generating something, but it had not learned to map the input to actual pronunciation.This was our first fine-tuning attempt, and the results reflect that.

cards.title: The failure very likely stems fr…
cards.body: The failure very likely stems from a combination of two factors:

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: **Not enough data, spread across…
cards.body: **Not enough data, spread across too many voices**: Teaching F5-TTS an entirely new language typically requires 50 to 100+ hours of audio.

cards.title: DarijaTTS-clean contains around …
cards.body: DarijaTTS-clean contains around 15-20 hours — already short of that threshold.More importantly, that audio is spread across many different speakers.

cards.title: A multi-speaker dataset forces t…
cards.body: A multi-speaker dataset forces the model to simultaneously learn the language and adapt to speaker variation, which makes convergence harder.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: A single-voice dataset of the sa…
cards.body: A single-voice dataset of the same size would likely have given the model a much better chance, since it could focus entirely on learning Darija phonetics without the added complexity.

cards.title: **Unvocalized text**: DarijaTTS-…
cards.body: **Unvocalized text**: DarijaTTS-clean uses Arabic script without tashkeel.

cards.title: Tashkeel would give the model an…
cards.body: Tashkeel would give the model an explicit pronunciation target, but Moroccans do not write Darija with it in everyday life, making a diacritized dataset very hard to build — you cannot scrape it from real-world sources and manual annotation is costly.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: This alone definitely did not ca…
cards.body: This alone definitely did not cause the failure, but it is a known source of input ambiguity for any TTS model trained on Arabic-script Darija data.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: **Data quantity and speaker dive…
cards.body: **Data quantity and speaker diversity matter as much as the model.

cards.title: ** Teaching F5-TTS a new languag…
cards.body: ** Teaching F5-TTS a new language requires significantly more data than we had — and ideally from a single speaker or a small consistent set, so the model can focus on learning the language rather than simultaneously adapting to many different voices.

cards.title: DarijaTTS-clean's 15-20 hours sp…
cards.body: DarijaTTS-clean's 15-20 hours spread across multiple speakers was not enough for convergence.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: **The text input matters as much…
cards.body: **The text input matters as much as the model.** Getting the acoustic architecture right is necessary but not sufficient.

cards.title: If the text representation canno…
cards.body: If the text representation cannot fully specify pronunciation, the model cannot learn the mapping.

cards.title: For Darija, the missing piece is…
cards.body: For Darija, the missing piece is a G2P layer that handles how the language is actually written — unvocalized Arabic or Arabizi — rather than expecting a sanitized, diacritized input that no one produces in practice.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: **A negative result is still a r…
cards.body: **A negative result is still a result.** We know exactly where DarijaTTS-clean fails for F5-TTS fine-tuning.We know what a better dataset looks like.

cards.title: The vocabulary extension and emb…
cards.body: The vocabulary extension and embedding resize procedures work correctly — those are reusable building blocks.The failure is localized and diagnosable.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: A longer-term direction could be…
cards.body: A longer-term direction could be a grapheme-to-phoneme model for Darija, which would normalize written text into a phoneme sequence before passing it to the synthesis model.

cards.title: This is harder than it sounds: A…
cards.body: This is harder than it sounds: Arabic script and Arabizi are structurally different enough that a single G2P model covering both would be noisy and complex to train — separate models per script are probably more realistic, and each one is already a non-trivial task on its own.

cards.title: That said, the payoff would be s…
cards.body: That said, the payoff would be significant, especially for loanwords: French words like "voiture" or "problème", Amazigh terms, and proper names all get transliterated inconsistently in Arabic script, and a G2P layer would resolve them to the correct Moroccan-inflected pronunciation rather than a broken Arabic reading.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: We did not ship a working Darija…
cards.body: We did not ship a working Darija TTS system. We built the infrastructure, identified exactly why the first attempt failed, and pointed at what needs to change.

cards.title: The main pipeline — Chinese-to-E…
cards.body: The main pipeline — Chinese-to-English video transformation — worked. The voices were cloned. The timing held. The videos were watchable.

cards.title: The Darija extension was a shot …
cards.body: The Darija extension was a shot at something harder. It taught us more.

pages.title: From Chinese to English — and a Detour into Moroccan Arabic: Building an AI Video Language Transformation Pipeline

cards.title: *Adam Maytoussi and Soulaymane T…
cards.body: *Adam Maytoussi and Soulaymane Temimi are students at UTSEUS. The full pipeline code is available on [GitHub](https://github.com/EmporioSabo/ai-video-language-transformation).*
